title: Revenue Drop Analysis
business_definition: Revenue is the total monetary value of sales in a period. A drop means fewer orders, lower order values, or higher refunds — each requiring a different fix.
diagnostic_questions: Did order volume drop or did average order value drop?
Is the drop concentrated in a specific region, segment, or product category?
Did refunds spike in the same period?
Did new customer acquisition slow, or did existing customers spend less?
Is this a data quality issue — missing orders, timezone shift, duplicate cancellations?

causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS orders, AVG(amount) AS aov, SUM(amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

causal_relationships.symptom: revenue drop concentrated in one region or segment
causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT region, DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS orders, SUM(amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

causal_relationships.symptom: revenue drop driven by fewer new customers
causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', first_order.first_date) AS cohort_month, COUNT(*) AS new_customers FROM (SELECT customer_id, MIN(order_date) AS first_date FROM orders GROUP BY 1) first_order GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

inflation_causes.cause: Cancelled or refunded orders still counted in revenue
inflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT order_status, COUNT(*), SUM(amount) FROM orders GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

inflation_causes.cause: Test or internal orders inflating revenue figures
inflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT is_test, SUM(amount) FROM orders WHERE is_test IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1

deflation_causes.cause: Revenue recognition delay — orders created but not yet captured
deflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT COUNT(*), SUM(amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending' AND order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

deflation_causes.cause: Currency conversion applied inconsistently across periods
deflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT currency, COUNT(*), SUM(amount) FROM orders WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY 1

anti_patterns: Do not compare raw revenue across different time zones without normalising to UTC
Do not include pending or draft orders in finalised revenue figures

sql_assets.revenue_by_period: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS revenue, COUNT(*) AS orders, AVG(amount) AS aov FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
